newsSince: At its June 12 AGM Walsin's management reiterated that AI-server demand is unquenchable and passive-component supply tightness will persist into 2028, and is tripling capex to ~NT$3B to expand MLCC/resistor capacity 10-15%. Industry-wide, June saw Samsung Electro-Mechanics suspend MLCC quotes and high-cap AI-server MLCC spot prices up 50-60%, confirming the bottleneck pricing cycle. This is genuine fundamental reinforcement, though the stock is now +363% YTD and limit-up since June 9.

verdicts.rankRationale: AGM (Jun 12) + June MLCC price action (Samsung Electro-Mechanics quote freeze, +50-60% high-cap AI MLCC, capex tripled to NT$3B, crunch to 2028) genuinely strengthen the bottleneck thesis. But on a residual-alpha page this is now consensus and richly priced; thesis-in-the-price, so hold rank vs peers.
verdicts.bull: Confirmed multi-year AI MLCC supply crunch to 2028; pricing power and tripled capex make Walsin a core passive bottleneck beneficiary.
verdicts.neutral: Bottleneck real but now widely recognized; +363% YTD means the alpha is largely captured.
verdicts.bear: Hidden-gem premise gone; crowded, momentum-driven, vulnerable to any demand-pull-forward unwind.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: AI-server MLCC price hikes sustaining through 2H26-2028.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding/demand pull-forward reversal after a parabolic run.
